title: Gymnocactus knuthianus
intro: Gymnocactus knuthianus, also known as Mammillaria knuthiana, is a species of cactus native to Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnocactus knuthianus include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its use requires further scientific investigation and standardization.
taxonomy: Gymnocactus knuthianus is a species within the cactus family (Cactaceae). It is a monotypic genus, meaning it consists of a single species. The genus Gymnocactus was later merged with Mammillaria, and Gymnocactus knuthianus is now classified as Mammillaria knuthiana. However, some sources still recognize it as Gymnocactus knuthianus. The classification can vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a type of mammillaria.
uses: Gymnocactus knuthianus, also known as Mammillaria knuthiana, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Gymnocactus knuthianus is said to be effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing fever. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its supposed antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal efficacy of Gymnocactus knuthianus.

parts: Gymnocactus knuthianus, also known as the Thimble Cactus, contains medicinal properties that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's latex, which is the milky sap extracted from the plant's stems or leaves, is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
